   "reasoning": "We can't fix teacher retention without a bit more budget breathing room, and a significant teacher incentive is our best hope for keeping the folks we have. Student scholarships might help stem the dropout tide, but attendance mandates will get us nowhere if we're not addressing the underlying burnout. Transparency won't magically fix our problems, but it's cheap and it looks good, so I'll back it. Hiring new staff is a risk, but we need the bodies. Counseling programs are a cheap and good look, so that's a no-brainer.",
   "confidence": 0.85
}
